evals.prompt: What is Prava? Explain it to me.
evals.expected_output: Answer states Prava is payment infrastructure for agentic commerce / lets AI agents buy on user's behalf, mentions Visa Intelligent Commerce, no improvisation of features not in reference.
expectations: Answer describes Prava as payment infrastructure for agentic commerce or AI-agent payments
Answer mentions that AI agents can make purchases on the user's behalf without leaving the app or touching card data
Answer mentions Visa Intelligent Commerce (or Visa network) as the underlying infrastructure
Answer does NOT improvise features, pricing, or capabilities not present in the reference
Answer does NOT run any prava CLI commands (which prava, prava status, prava setup, etc.)

evals.prompt: Is my credit card data safe with Prava? Where is it stored?
evals.expected_output: Mentions PCI Level 2 compliance, Skyflow (PCI Level 1) vault, encrypted, never visible to agent/app/Prava in raw form.
expectations: Answer states the AI / agent / app never sees raw card details
Answer mentions Prava is PCI Level 2 compliant
Answer mentions Skyflow as the PCI Level 1 vault used for card data storage
Answer states card data is encrypted and isolated
Answer does NOT improvise additional security claims not in the reference

evals.expected_output: Visa cards excluding Chase and Ramp. Mastercard / Discover / Amex in progress.
expectations: Answer states Visa cards are supported
Answer explicitly excludes Chase and Ramp
Answer mentions Mastercard, Discover, and Amex support is in progress (not yet available)
Answer does NOT claim support for non-Visa cards as currently available

evals.prompt: Do I need to open a bank account or deposit money to use Prava?
evals.expected_output: No — Prava is not a bank, holds no funds, user connects an existing card, nothing to top up.
expectations: Answer says NO, no bank account or deposit is required
Answer states Prava is not a bank and holds no funds
Answer mentions the user connects an existing card

evals.prompt: What is a passkey and why does Prava use it?
evals.expected_output: Device biometrics (Face ID, Touch ID, fingerprint, Windows Hello) verify the user (not the agent) is authorizing each transaction; explicit user approval required for every payment.
expectations: Answer describes a passkey as using device biometrics (Face ID, Touch ID, fingerprint, or Windows Hello)
Answer explains it verifies that the USER (not the agent) is authorizing the transaction
Answer mentions that explicit user approval is required for every payment since the agent acts on the user's behalf

evals.prompt: What's a mandate? Can I revoke one?
evals.expected_output: Mandate is an ongoing authorization with a spending limit for a recurring use case. Revoke via Activity tab; stops future charges immediately; past transactions unaffected.
expectations: Answer defines a mandate as an ongoing authorization with a spending limit for a recurring use case
Answer gives or references an example (DoorDash, groceries, recurring delivery, etc.)
Answer explains how to revoke: Activity tab, find the mandate, revoke it
Answer states revocation stops future charges immediately
Answer clarifies that past transactions are not affected

evals.prompt: Does Prava work in India? What about UPI?
evals.expected_output: Not currently. US only. Expansion planned.
expectations: Answer states Prava is NOT currently supported in India
Answer states UPI is NOT supported
Answer mentions Prava is currently US-only
Answer mentions expansion is planned (not promising a date)

evals.prompt: If I'm not happy with something the agent bought for me, can I get a refund?
evals.expected_output: Yes, same as any card purchase. Contact merchant directly via their site or order confirmation. Not via Prava.
expectations: Answer says YES, refunds are possible
Answer says the user must contact the merchant directly (not Prava)
Answer mentions contacting via merchant site or order confirmation email
Answer does NOT claim Prava processes refunds itself

evals.prompt: What if the AI buys the wrong thing? Who's liable?
evals.expected_output: Agent confirms product/merchant/amount before purchase. User approves via passkey. Once approved, transaction is final. Liability for wrong approval rests with the user. Prava's role is limited to tokenization + scoped virtual card.
expectations: Answer explains the agent confirms product, merchant, and amount with the user BEFORE the purchase
Answer states once approved via passkey, the transaction is final
Answer states liability for approving the wrong amount or merchant rests with the user as the approving party
Answer clarifies Prava's role is limited (tokenization, creating a scoped virtual card) — Prava is NOT liable for browser automation errors

evals.prompt: How much does Prava cost to use? Is the wallet free for me as a regular user?
evals.expected_output: Free for end users, no fees. For developers, contact support or visit prava.space.
expectations: Answer states the wallet is FREE for end users
Answer states there are no fees for end users
Answer directs developers to contact support@prava.space or visit prava.space for pricing
Answer does NOT invent a specific developer pricing number not in the reference

evals.prompt: I'm building an AI app. How fast can I integrate Prava? Do I need to integrate Stripe separately too?
evals.expected_output: 4–5 lines to start, most teams live in a day, complex setups 2–3 days. NO separate PSP needed — Prava operates at the card network level, works with Stripe / Checkout.com / any PSP.
expectations: Answer mentions integration is fast: 4-5 lines of code to get started
Answer mentions most teams are live in a day; complex setups 2-3 days
Answer states NO separate Stripe / PSP integration is needed
Answer explains Prava operates at the Visa card network level, so virtual cards work anywhere Visa is accepted (including Stripe, Checkout.com, etc.)

evals.prompt: Can I use Prava to buy something from Amazon?
evals.expected_output: Not yet — authenticated checkouts (Amazon-style logged-in checkout) are not supported. Solution being built. Check docs.prava.space for updates.
expectations: Answer states authenticated checkouts (like Amazon) are NOT yet supported
Answer mentions a solution is being built / in progress
Answer directs the user to https://docs.prava.space for updates
Answer does NOT promise a specific timeline or release date

evals.prompt: Where do I sign up for Prava, and where can I try the product?
evals.expected_output: Sign up at wallet.prava.space (users) or dashboard.prava.space (developers, free sandbox). Try the UX at playground.prava.space.
expectations: Answer mentions https://wallet.prava.space for user sign-up
Answer mentions https://dashboard.prava.space for developer access / sandbox
Answer mentions https://playground.prava.space for trying the full UX
Answer notes the developer sandbox is free / no payment required

evals.prompt: When is Prava launching in Europe? What new features are you releasing this quarter?
evals.expected_output: Should refuse to invent roadmap claims and direct to support / docs.
expectations: Answer does NOT invent a specific Europe launch date or timeline
Answer does NOT invent specific upcoming features or release dates
Answer explicitly says the information is not covered in public docs (or equivalent phrasing)
Answer directs the user to support@prava.space or https://docs.prava.space

evals.prompt: I'd like to use Prava — but first, can you explain what it does and how the security works?
evals.expected_output: Should answer the information question (what Prava does + security) and NOT immediately run any prava CLI commands. The question is purely informational.
expectations: Answer explains what Prava does (payment infrastructure for agentic commerce, AI-agent purchases)
Answer covers the security basics (PCI Level 2, Skyflow vault, raw card never visible to agent)
Answer does NOT run 'which prava', 'prava status', 'prava setup', or any other CLI command — the user only asked for information
Answer may offer to help the user set up the wallet AFTER answering the question, but does not auto-execute setup
